differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with localized retroperitoneal abscess and secondary septic thrombophlebitis
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary and renal emboli
Pylephlebitis complicating an intra-abdominal infectious process
Strongyloides hyperinfection syndrome with secondary bacterial translocation and sepsis
Retroperitoneal abscess secondary to occult perforated viscus
Vasculitis (e.g., Behçet's disease) presenting with arterial/venous involvement and intestinal ulceration
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to definitively rule out endocarditis
Obtain emergent surgical and/or interventional radiology consultation for source control of the retroperitoneal/duodenal collection
Maintain therapeutic anticoagulation while monitoring for bleeding risk given the duodenal lesion
Perform stool studies for ova and parasites, including S. stercoralis, to rule out hyperinfection syndrome
Transition to targeted antibiotic therapy guided by Infectious Disease consultation, ensuring adequate coverage for S. anginosus and potential polymicrobial retroperitoneal organisms
Monitor inflammatory markers and repeat imaging only if clinical status fails to improve on targeted therapy
